items.title: 'Canary in coal mine' warning as marine pest found in new area

children.content: The discovery on Tasmania's west coast has scientists and fishers alike fearing the spread of a destructive species to another previously untouched part of the state's marine environment.

items.title: Workers at embattled smelter face last payday amid calls for certainty

children.content: As what could be their last payday looms, an alliance of unions says more should be done to ensure stability for 200 workers at Australia's only manganese smelter.

image.altText: A steel worker in highly protective gear working on hot steel products.

items.title: 'Highly inappropriate': Parliament questions election funding promise for at-risk youth program

children.content: The taxpayer-funded outreach organisation JCP Youth runs the "flagship" BEAST program to divert at-risk youths, but a parliamentary committee has concerns over its evidence base and lack of a tender process for funding.

image.altText: Young people climb across a rope wall suspended over a muddy creek.

items.title: New images released with Hobart stadium precinct master plan

children.content: A master plan for the precinct surrounding the future AFL stadium in Hobart has been unveiled, while the ABC understands the company behind New Zealand's premier stadium in Christchurch is among those competing to build it.

gallery.altText: An aerial view od Hobart's waterfront, with an artist's impression of the stadium and new housing.

items.title: Ukraine looking to 'capitalise' on new momentum against Russia

children.content: Western officials and analysts say Ukrainian forces have disrupted Russia's planned spring offensive and managed to retake territory.

image.altText: A man standing by the ruins of a bombed out car in a car park.

items.title: Proposal to block non-citizens from first homebuyer scheme slammed

children.content: Multicultural groups are concerned Angus Taylor's proposal to prevent permanent residents from accessing the Australian Government 5% Deposit Scheme could deter skilled migrants from coming to Australia.

image.altText: Angus Taylor

items.title: Woman suffers brain injury after receiving 'toxic dose' of wrong drug

children.content: A 36-year-old woman who says she suffered a "hypoxic brain injury" after being given a "toxic dose" of the wrong drug during routine surgery at Palmerston Regional Hospital is suing the Northern Territory government for negligence.

items.title: Dozens of workers with disability made redundant by award-winning charity

children.content: Help Enterprises made 41 positions redundant weeks after it was named Brisbane's social enterprise of the year.

image.altText: A close up of a man's hands in his lap.

items.title: Kerr scores landmark goal as Matildas win FIFA Series

children.content: Sam Kerr's 75th international goal has helped the Matildas to a surprisingly tough, controversial 2-0 victory over Kenya in the FIFA Series final.

items.title: SA government apologises to prisoner denied abortion medication

children.content: The South Australian ombudsman calls for changes to medical monitoring at the Adelaide Women's Prison after a female prisoner was denied a medical abortion, which the health minister says is unacceptable.

items.title: Short-stay levy overperforms, as holiday rental growth stagnates in Victoria

children.content: Data suggests the growth in short-stay accommodation listings in Victoria has slowed in the year after the Victorian government's new tax came into effect.

items.title: Adam was quoted $60,000 for his home insurance. His local council is taking action

children.content: Adam Osborne was quoted a staggering $60,000 for his home insurance this year. With others in the same boat, six Queensland councils are looking at creating their own insurance mutual to drive down premiums.

items.title: A lucky chat, then a lifeline for men with prostate cancer

children.content: Michael Platts panicked when he was diagnosed with prostate cancer. Now he wants to help others break through the stigma and silence surrounding the condition.

items.title: Henderson 'content' knowing her epic reign will not last forever

items.title: Defence spending to climb by $53 billion as 'international norms' erode

children.content: Australia's defence spending will climb by $53 billion over the next decade amid what the federal government says are "intensifying" global risks.

image.altText: Australian defence force 2024-10-22 11:10:00

items.title: At least nine dead, including eight children, in Türkiye school shooting

children.content: A boy, believed to be aged about 13, carried out the shooting in southeastern Türkiye, killing his fellow students and a teacher. It is the second school shooting in the country in two days.

items.title: Taylor's migration plan doesn't make much sense. It doesn't have to

children.content: VicGrid escalates its attempts to access land for VNI West surveys, threatening fines or court-ordered entry to farmers who stand in the way.

items.title: How the technology we use every day is changing our brains

children.content: If it feels harder to concentrate than it used to, it's not just you. Scientists say your brain is reacting exactly as it was designed to — but in a very different world.

items.title: Hospital review identifies series of errors before man's death by suicide

children.content: Blake Whittington, 23, was taken to hospital after a suicide attempt. He was discharged, then referred to a support service that did not contact him until days after he took his own life.

items.title: Media boss's wife feared she would die, tore his clothing during alleged attack, court told

children.content: The wife of media executive Antony Catalano fled the couple's luxury apartment and flagged down a passing car for help following a sustained attack by her husband that caused hospitalisation, police allege.

image.altText: A side profile shot of Australian Community Media executive chairman Antony Catalano in an outdoor setting.

items.title: Which shade of white is right? Dentists explain the teeth colours

items.title: Jury hears 'no bad blood' before sistergirl's alleged fatal stabbing

items.title: Here's what you need to know about the Gina Rinehart court ruling

children.content: The blockbuster court ruling over mining tenements worth billions of dollars in WA's iron ore rich Pilbara is the culmination of a complicated legal battle spanning more than a decade that has pitted family against family and siblings against each other.

articles.title: Split decision as Gina Rinehart, Wright family both claim legal wins

children.content: Gina Rinehart is dealt a blow, with a court finding the family of her father's former business partner has the right to potentially hundreds of millions of dollars in royalties from lucrative Pilbara mining assets.
